nodes.label: Capability-Based Microkernel Transition
nodes.rationale: Deliberate architectural migration from monolithic ring-0 service layer to capability-based microkernel with externalised VFS/drivers/block I/O as Ring 3 servers communicating via IPC capabilities. Current monolithic code is structured as isolated subsystems with clean interface boundaries to make extraction a wrapping exercise, not a rewrite.

nodes.label: Freestanding C++20 Kernel
nodes.rationale: No libc, no libstdc++, no runtime — zero-overhead object-oriented kernel design, compile-time type safety, deterministic execution. C++20 Concepts for compile-time validation, constexpr/consteval for static scheduling parameters, RAII for all kernel resources, noexcept by default with -fno-exceptions.

nodes.label: ResourceTracker Leak Detection Principle
nodes.rationale: Architecture over features — a new driver does not ship without a test, a ResourceTracker entry, and a documented failure mode.

nodes.label: VFS Touched Flag (Test Isolation Optimization)
nodes.rationale: ~95% of tests never touch VFS but pay full daemon death+ELF-load+remount cost (~150k cycles per test). Option B introduces per-test vfs_touched flag: if clear, skip reload_daemon_tasks() and reset_and_remount(), only rebuild ready queue. Estimated 70-80% reduction in per-test overhead, full 'all' suite expected to drop from ~45s to ~15-20s.
